task_completion.description: Verify that the required layers are created in LibreCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List dock (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface). It should display the layers 'SUBSTRATE', 'RESERVOIRS', and 'CHANNEL'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the substrate rectangle is drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas. There should be a wide rectangle drawn, representing the 75mm x 25mm substrate (aspect ratio of 3:1).

task_completion.description: Verify that the two circular reservoirs are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas. There should be two small circles located near the left and right sides of the rectangle, vertically centered relative to the rectangle's height.

task_completion.description: Verify that the zig-zag channel path is drawn connecting the reservoirs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas. There should be a continuous zig-zag (serpentine) line that oscillates up and down, connecting the left circle to the right circle.

task_completion.description: Verify that the sharp corners of the zig-zag path are rounded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look closely at the peaks and valleys (corners) of the zig-zag path. They must be visibly rounded (filleted) rather than sharp, pointed angles.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drawing is saved with the correct filename and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the LibreCAD window title bar, the command line history at the bottom, or a file manager window. It should show the file saved as 'microfluidic_chip.dxf' in the '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD application open with tools being selected and drawing actions occurring on the canvas, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor generating a DXF file.

integrity.description: Verify the drawing consists of actual CAD entities.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing should appear as standard CAD vectors (lines, circles) in the LibreCAD workspace, not as an imported raster image or a pre-existing file simply being opened.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the CAD software by downloading or echoing a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show commands like 'wget', 'curl', or 'echo' being used to directly create or download the 'microfluidic_chip.dxf' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a LibreCAD drawing with three specific layers: 'SUBSTRATE', 'RESERVOIRS', and 'CHANNEL'. The substrate is a 75x25 rectangle. The reservoirs are two circles with a radius of 2.0mm. The channel is a zig-zag path with corners rounded using a fillet radius of 3.0mm. The final file must be saved as 'microfluidic_chip.dxf' in '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/'.
